question: Declare an array of 4 integers. Initialize it with some values. Then, use a pointer to the first element to iterate through the array and print each element using pointer arithmetic.

test_cases.expected:
10
20
30
40
[Remaining output will be garbage values or program will crash due to undefined behavior from out-of-bounds memory access.]

errors: The loop condition `i < sizeof(arr)` is semantically incorrect. `sizeof(arr)` returns the size of the array in bytes (e.g., 16 for 4 integers), not the number of elements. This causes the loop to iterate far beyond the array's bounds, leading to out-of-bounds memory access and undefined behavior.
